chapter_title.ar: باب الرَّجُلِ يَؤُمُّ النِّسَاءَ وَ الْمَرْأَةِ تَؤُمُّ النِّسَاءَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 52 – The man leading the women, and the woman leading the women

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح على الظاهر - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/262)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح على الظاهر - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/262)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الْمَرْأَةِ تَؤُمُّ النِّسَاءَ فَقَالَ إِذَا كُنَّ جَمِيعاً أَمَّتْهُنَّ فِي النَّافِلَةِ فَأَمَّا الْمَكْتُوبَةُ فَلَا وَ لَا تَقَدَّمْهُنَّ وَ لَكِنْ تَقُومُ وَسَطاً مِنْهُنَّ .

en.hubeali: A group, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from Fazalat, from Ibn Sinan, from Suleyman Bin Khalid who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullahasws about the woman leading the women (in Salāt). So heasws said: ‘When they have gathered, she can lead them in the optional (Salāt). But, as for the Obligatory (Salāt), so no; and she should not preceded them (to be in front), but she should be standing in the middle of them’.
en.sarwar: 2. A group has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Husayn ibn Sa‘id from Fadalah from ibn Sinan from Sulayman ibn Khalid who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a woman’s leading other women in Salah (prayer). He (the Imam) said, ‘She can do so if they all follow her in optional Salah (prayer), but not in obligatory Salah (prayer), and she is not to lead them, instead she stands up in their middle.’”
